pages.raw_text:
City councilmen, members OF the
board Of public works and the city
engineer this morning locked horns
on the bridge question. Standing
upon the partly finished structure
that will replace the collapsed mid
die span of the Howard street
bridge, Councilmen Gray, Funk and
Baldwin on one side got their
backs up on the proposition Of En-
gineer Mcintyre, Mayor Daggett
and Commissioners ONO. Roy and
Weymouth to- put the new steel
bridge that now stands unused in
the cars in place of the old Haver-
male bridge.


Those favoring this plan want
the new bridge put in so that it
will make crotch practically in
the same position as the present
Havermale bridge, but with the
north side of 1t intersecting the
middle span 40 feet north from
where it does now. This plan is
also favored by the Washington
Water Power company. ....


The councilmen's proposition Is
to do away entirely with rne Haver-
male bridge and use the new one
ordered in its place as the middle
span in place of concrete.


The objection offered by the
board to this proposition was that
it would necessitate Havermale
street striking the middle span at
right angles, thus creating a con-
gestion OF traffic on account OF the
sharp angle. Traffic north and
south on Howard street and west
on Havermale street would meet
and on account of the sharp turn
would be at a disadvantage for all.


The councilmen in backing up
their proposition said a fill could
be made at small cost and a great
saving would be made by the city
In putting in the new $23. 900 steel
structure for the middle span,
